BUILDING IN PUBLIC IS SCARY. DO IT ANYWAY
articles.summary: Polished, infrequent launches slow digital teams down, while building in public creates momentum and trust through steady shipping and open communication. The approach works when builders share updates often and stay focused on real user needs.

articles.title: NETFLIX AND THE HOLLYWOOD END GAME
articles.summary: Netflix's rise shows that Internet distribution, not content, holds the real power. Buying Warner Bros. is a bid to own the IP it boosts. Netflix's biggest threat now is YouTube and the surge of user-generated content competing for attention.

articles.title: WHY LABELING RELATIONSHIPS IS SO IMPORTANT
articles.summary: Companies default to tidy hierarchies, but real operating systems behave like complex networks. When you map the real relationships, you uncover how strategy and execution actually work.

articles.title: THE CINDERELLA “GLASS SLIPPER” EFFECT
articles.summary: A few AI models achieve instant, lasting retention by perfectly solving a high-value workload for early users, creating a “Glass Slipper” cohort that never leaves. This raises the stakes on launches: teams must deliver a real capability breakthrough, because the first perfect fit wins.

articles.title: BUILDING PRODUCTS FROM ZERO: WHAT REALLY MATTERS WHEN YOU'RE THE FIRST PM
articles.summary: Listen early, define principles, move fast, and trust qualitative insight before metrics settle. Staying close to customers and documenting choices builds a solid product foundation that can grow with the company.

articles.title: THIS IS HOW TO FIND MEANING IN LIFE: 6 SECRETS FROM RESEARCH
articles.summary: Meaning is something we build through values, connection, awe, work, and the stories we craft about our lives. It appears when we show up consistently, even when life feels ordinary or hard.

articles.title: THE PROBLEM WITH PRODUCT REVIEWS
articles.summary: Product reviews often focus on small fixes instead of clarifying whether the problem matters or the product can succeed. Companies equate speed with coding quickly, so reviews become checkpoints instead of sources of real insight.

articles.title: CAN WE DRIVE THE SAME OUTCOME FOR DIFFERENT CUSTOMER SEGMENTS?
articles.summary: Repeat outcomes when doing so reveals whether segments need different solutions or metrics, because one shared statement often hides real differences.

articles.title: WHY AI MAKES EXPERTS MORE VALUABLE THAN EVER
articles.summary: AI gives the most leverage to people who already know their field well and can guide and correct its output.
